_comment: F1-S9 slice 2 (issue #12): the mutation-testing baseline for snowflake/assert_dev_ci_grants.py + snowflake/check_forbidden_grants.py + snowflake/validate_migrations.py (the last added in the ready round -- a Codex finding: check_forbidden_grants.py directly calls validate_migrations.find_candidate_migration_files to decide which files get scanned for a forbidden PUBLIC grant, so it's genuinely part of the grant-boundary security surface, not unrelated filename/render validation). Re-measured on main via `mutmut run` + `mutmut export-cicd-stats` (19 Jul 2026, mutmut==3.6.0) after widening the scope -- deterministic across repeated runs. check_mutation_score.py fails the check if a later run's killed/(killed+survived) ratio drops below this baseline's ratio, OR if total drops below this baseline's total (a shrinking mutant count with an unchanged/improved ratio is itself suspicious -- see check_mutation_score.py's own module docstring). Recorded as raw counts (not a pre-computed percentage) so a reviewer can recompute the ratio by hand and so a future re-baseline (after genuinely strengthening the test suite, or a legitimate scope change like this one) is an honest, auditable count update, not an opaque number. Per the operator scoping decision on issue #12: this starts at whatever the surface's REAL baseline is, not an aspirational target -- 577 mutants, 421 killed, 156 survived (~72.96% mutation score) on this pass.
